description: In SQL, when object dependencies (views, foreign keys, triggers) and migration ordering change, verify the lockfile, generated artifacts, and a runtime smoke.
body: In SQL, a dependency change ripples through object dependencies (views, foreign keys, triggers) and migration ordering, generated bindings, and runtime behavior, so it needs more than a recompile. Use when object dependencies (views, foreign keys, triggers) and migration ordering are added, removed, or version-bumped. Do not merge a dependency bump on a green compile alone, and avoid editing the lockfile by hand. Actions: inspect the dependency delta, re-resolve and review object dependencies (views, foreign keys, triggers) and migration ordering, regenerate affected ORM-generated DDL and migration files emitted by the schema tool, then run a transaction that runs the statement, asserts row counts, then rolls back and smoke the import/runtime path. Validation signals include a reviewed lockfile diff, a clean EXPLAIN ANALYZE, the query plan, and index-usage inspection pass, and a passing runtime smoke. Risk boundary: escalate on a major-version bump, a transitive conflict you cannot resolve, or a license/security flag.
